title: V2.6 Scenario Coverage and duplicate-key rejection

sources.reason: Defines the current Governance Compression model and Cockpit status workflow.
Defines the repository terms and architectural boundaries for Governance Compression and Cockpit Status.
Defines the path-based coverage guard rules that must remain aligned with the new Scenario Coverage validators.
Defines the hard-risk policy for Scenario Coverage warnings and hard gates.
Installer must ship the new Scenario Coverage guard script into generated repositories.
Documents the Contract and Summary field model that must be extended for scenario coverage.
Provides the reviewer-facing Cockpit interpretation that must reflect Scenario Coverage.
The example Contract template must be updated to show the new optional field.
The example Summary template must be updated to show the new optional field and evidence rules.

problemStatement: Medium and high risk Work Items need a generic way to prove which risk-domain scenarios were verified, which remain unverified, and which do not apply, without hard-coding domain libraries into Core.

riskAssessment.reason: This task changes shared governance schemas, adds a new guard, and updates Cockpit status derivation without changing product code.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit for a bounded V2.6 governance update.

preReviewWarnings: Review the duplicate-key loader behavior on active Contract/Summary JSON first.
Check that Scenario Coverage stays generic and does not bake release/auth scenario libraries into Core.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record checkpoints before and after the edit set to keep the V2.6 schema and status changes explainable.

acceptance: Duplicate keys in active Contract and Summary JSON fail with file-path and repeated-key diagnostics.
Scenario Coverage is validated as an optional shared structure with verified, unverified, and not_applicable rules.
A dedicated `make check-ai-scenario-coverage` target reports scenario coverage findings and feeds Cockpit status.
Cockpit status exposes a Scenario Coverage signal that distinguishes complete, incomplete, not_required, and unknown states.
Templates, docs, and tests are updated so V2.6 remains generic and backward compatible for low-risk Work Items.
guidelines: Keep Scenario Coverage mechanism generic; scenario content stays in the Work Item, not in Core policy catalogs.
Document new public commands and user-facing governance fields.

intent.problem: Medium and high risk Work Items need a generic way to prove which risk-domain scenarios were verified, which remain unverified, and which do not apply, without hard-coding domain libraries into Core.
constraints: Keep the implementation backward compatible for low-risk Work Items that do not need Scenario Coverage.
Reject duplicate keys in active governance JSON so repeated fields cannot hide or override required evidence.
Keep scenario content project-owned and avoid introducing release/auth-specific scenario catalogs into the template.
intent.rationale: A small schema and guard extension can surface risk-domain coverage in the existing Governance Compression model while preserving AI Cockpit's language-agnostic, repository-owned design.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly requested the V2.6 governance update across .ai governance files and Makefile entrypoints.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
